name: Madagascar
avoid: November to March brings cyclone risk, washed-out tracks, and humid rainforest walks
Domestic schedules can shift without much notice, so do not stack long-haul and island flights on the same day
Many west and south routes are punishing overland unless you budget for a private 4x4
stopoverNote: Antananarivo is the essential hub: nearly every practical Madagascar Airlines domestic routing touches TNR, so keeping one buffer night in the capital protects the rest of the trip.

cities.name: Antananarivo
cities.notes: Hilltop palaces, markets, museums, and the main international/domestic gateway before heading into rainforest, baobab country, or the coast

cities.name: Andasibe
cities.notes: Best-known base for hearing indri calls at dawn, guided rainforest walks, night wildlife spotting, and easy access to Analamazaotra and Mantadia circuits

cities.name: Morondava
cities.notes: The west-coast launch point for Avenue of the Baobabs sunsets, Kirindy wildlife drives, and long dirt-road expeditions into Menabe country

cities.name: Isalo National Park
cities.notes: Ranohira-based access to sandstone canyons, natural pools, ring-tailed lemurs, and some of Madagascar’s best dry-season trekking

cities.name: Nosy Be
cities.notes: Island finish for Lokobe forest, marine reserve snorkeling, sunset viewpoints, and easier beach downtime after the overland-heavy mainland sections

itinerary.sim: Telma is the safest Madagascar-wide choice once you leave Antananarivo; Orange Madagascar is fine in bigger towns and Nosy Be. Expect roughly ₹450–₹900 for a tourist SIM or eSIM with enough data for maps, WhatsApp, and flight changes.

days.theme: Use the optional extra island day for diving, whale season, or simply to buffer the famously loose domestic flight network

activities.name: Intro scuba or two-tank dive from Nosy Be

Airport transfer and baggage buffer for domestic departure

connections.method: Taxi-brousse via Moramanga is the cheapest move, but a private 4x4 is far easier if you want door-to-door timing and stops
Return by road to Antananarivo, then connect onto a Madagascar Airlines / Tsaradia domestic; forcing this overland all the way west wastes too much time
This is one of the classic Madagascar pain points: either commit to a long private 4x4 repositioning or backtrack by air via Antananarivo/Toliara and finish by road to Ranohira
Road to Toliara followed by domestic flights, usually via Antananarivo, is the practical way to jump from the south-west canyons to the north-west islands
Scheduled Madagascar Airlines / Tsaradia domestic back to the capital for international departures

extras: April to October is the sweet spot: you get drier rainforest trails in Andasibe, much safer west-coast and south-west road conditions, and clearer beach weather around Nosy Be.
Madagascar National Parks entries and guide fees are usually cash-only in Ariary; carry small notes because remote gates rarely handle card payments or large bills well.
Guides are mandatory inside the MNP parks on this route, and the classic lemur night walks happen in community forests such as Mitsinjo or VOIMMA rather than inside the main park sectors.
Private 4x4 hire is the realistic comfort upgrade for Morondava, Kirindy, and Isalo logistics; taxi-brousse works best on the Antananarivo-Andasibe corridor if you are traveling light.
Madagascar Airlines domestic schedules can shift at short notice, so keep at least one buffer night in Antananarivo or Nosy Be before any long-haul departure.
Madagascar uses 220V European two-pin plugs, and even mid-range hotels outside the capital can have weak evening power or patchy hot water, so keep charging windows flexible.
